frame_analyses.description: Two needle drivers visible entering field, Penrose drain positioned on red platform with visible marks
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setup appears proper, drain positioned for suturing

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver grasping needle, left instrument positioning
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle appears loaded appropriately in right driver

frame_analyses.description: Both instruments working with needle and suture, preparing for placement
Needle being driven through Penrose drain at marked location
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle entry appears to be at appropriate location near marks

frame_analyses.description: Suture being pulled through drain, both marks appear to be engaged
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good technique, suture passing through both target areas

frame_analyses.description: Suture fully through drain, preparing for knot formation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean passage through drain, adequate suture length

frame_analyses.description: Hands working extracorporeally to form knot, suture ends visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Transition to extracorporeal technique, both suture ends accessible

frame_analyses.description: Continuing extracorporeal knot tying with both hands
Hands manipulating suture to form extracorporeal knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good hand coordination for knot formation

frame_analyses.description: Preparing knot pusher or using instrument to advance knot down
Knot being advanced down to tissue level
Task appears completed, suture cut and knot secured at drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completion with knot at appropriate level

confidence_rationale: Good visibility of key phases and technique. Camera angle provides clear view of suture placement and knot formation. Some uncertainty in exact mm measurements for suture deviation due to viewing angle, but placement appears accurate to marks.
cannot_determine: Exact mm deviation measurements - marks partially obscured by instruments in some frames
Precise knot pusher vs instrument technique - extracorporeal method clearly used but specific tools not always visible
technique_summary: Excellent expert-level performance with efficient needle loading, accurate suture placement, and smooth extracorporeal knot technique. Completion time of 75 seconds falls within expert benchmark range.
strengths: Very efficient needle loading and suture placement (30s total)
Accurate suture placement at both marks
improvement_suggestions: Extracorporeal knot formation could be slightly faster to achieve sub-60s expert times
Continue practicing to maintain this excellent technique consistency
